lines: Let's begin. [long pause] Wherever you are, whatever you've been doing — pause. [long pause] You've been given a few minutes to set things down. [long pause] Not forever. [long pause] Just for now.
Start with your breath. [long pause] We'll use a slow pattern — a long exhale that tells your nervous system it's safe to stand down.
Breathe in through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Hold gently... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven.

And exhale slowly through your mouth... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.
Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Hold... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven.

Out, slowly... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.
One more. [long pause] Inhale... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Hold... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven.

And release... [long pause] [long pause] all the way out... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.

Good. [long pause] Let your breathing return to whatever rhythm it finds. [long pause] Just notice how it feels to have slowed down, even for a moment. [long pause] Your body knows the difference. [long pause] Trust what it tells you.

Now listen to these words. [long pause] They were spoken to people who were exhausted — not lazy, not undisciplined, not weak. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] worn through. [long pause] The way you might be right now.

"Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. [long pause] Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. [long pause] For my yoke is easy and my burden is light."
Matthew eleven, verses twenty-eight through thirty.

You are carrying something right now. [long pause] Maybe you can name it — a deadline, a relationship that's draining you, a decision that won't stop circling, a responsibility that feels too large for one person. [long pause] Or maybe it's just a heaviness with no clear label. [long pause] A tiredness that sleep doesn't fix. [long pause] A low-grade exhaustion you've gotten so used to you've stopped noticing it.
Either way... [long pause] [long pause] you're invited to set it down. [long pause] Not permanently. [long pause] Not irresponsibly. [long pause] Just here. [long pause] Just for these few minutes.

And we're going to do that through your body. [long pause] Because stress doesn't just live in your thoughts — it buries itself in your muscles. [long pause] Your shoulders carry it. [long pause] Your jaw holds it. [long pause] Your stomach braces against it all day long. [long pause] So let's go there.

We're going to use a practice called progressive muscle relaxation. [long pause] The idea is simple: you deliberately tense a muscle group — hold it — and then release. [long pause] The release teaches your body what letting go actually feels like, because most of the time, you've forgotten.

Start with your hands. [long pause] Make fists. [long pause] Squeeze them tight — really tight. [long pause] Feel the tension in every finger, every knuckle, the muscles running up into your forearms. [long pause] Feel what it costs to grip this hard.
Keep holding... [long pause] [long pause] tighter... [long pause] [long pause]
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] one.
And release. [long pause] Open your hands completely. [long pause] Let them go soft. [long pause] Let them fall open.

Feel the difference. [long pause] That contrast — between gripping and letting go — that's what we're practicing with everything you're carrying. [long pause] The grip is familiar. [long pause] The release... [long pause] [long pause] you might have to relearn.
Now your arms. [long pause] Tense your biceps, your forearms — make them rigid, like you're bracing to lift something impossibly heavy. [long pause] Feel the effort. [long pause] The strain.
Hold that... [long pause] [long pause] feel every tight fiber... [long pause] [long pause]
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] one.
And release. [long pause] Let your arms go completely limp. [long pause] Heavy. [long pause] Useless. [long pause] Like they're made of warm sand, slowly pouring out.

Notice what release actually feels like in the muscle. [long pause] Not just the absence of tension — the warmth that follows. [long pause] The softness. [long pause] The quiet hum of a muscle that has remembered it's allowed to rest.

Your shoulders now. [long pause] Pull them up toward your ears. [long pause] High as they'll go. [long pause] This is the posture of stress — the shape your body makes when it's carrying too much. [long pause] When the world is too heavy and you're trying to hold it up with your trapezius muscles.

Hold it there. [long pause] All the weight of the day gathered in your shoulders. [long pause] All the things you're responsible for. [long pause] All the plates you're spinning. [long pause] Feel what it costs.
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] one.
And let them drop. [long pause] All at once. [long pause] Don't lower them carefully — just... [long pause] [long pause] drop.
Let gravity do the work. [long pause] Your shoulders have nowhere to be but down. [long pause] They've been up near your ears for hours, maybe days. [long pause] Let them rest.
"For my yoke is easy and my burden is light."

Your face now. [long pause] Scrunch everything — your forehead, your eyes, your nose, your mouth, your jaw. [long pause] Tight. [long pause] Like you're bracing for bad news. [long pause] Like someone's about to tell you something you don't want to hear. [long pause] This is what stress looks like in miniature, written across your face all day without you knowing.
Hold it... [long pause] [long pause] feel the strain behind your eyes, in your temples, along your jaw... [long pause] [long pause]
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] one.

And release. [long pause] Let your forehead smooth. [long pause] Let your eyes soften behind the lids. [long pause] Let your jaw come slightly open — teeth apart, tongue resting. [long pause] Let your whole face go slack and peaceful, like the face of someone sleeping.
Rest there. [long pause] Feel the quiet in your face. [long pause] The openness of a brow that's not furrowed.

Now your stomach. [long pause] Tighten your core — pull everything in, like you're bracing for impact. [long pause] This is what your body does when it's always waiting for the next hard thing. [long pause] Constant bracing. [long pause] Always prepared for a blow that may never come. [long pause] It's exhausting — not the blow itself, but the waiting.
Hold that tension. [long pause] Feel how tiring it is just to brace... [long pause] [long pause]
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] one.

And release. [long pause] Let your belly be soft. [long pause] Let it expand with your breath. [long pause] Let it be round and unguarded. [long pause] You don't need to brace right now. [long pause] Nothing is coming at you in this moment. [long pause] You are allowed to be undefended.
Let a breath fill your belly. [long pause] Feel it push against your loosened muscles. [long pause] Soft. [long pause] Easy. [long pause] This is how you were designed to breathe.

Your legs. [long pause] Tense everything — your thighs, your calves, your feet. [long pause] Push your heels into the floor. [long pause] Hard. [long pause] Like you're ready to run. [long pause] Like your body is preparing for fight or flight.
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] feel the energy stored in those muscles, the readiness, the coiled urgency... [long pause] [long pause]
Hold... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] one.
And release. [long pause] Let your legs go heavy. [long pause] Let them sink into whatever's supporting them. [long pause] Feel them become dead weight — gloriously, peacefully dead weight.

Good. [long pause] Now... [long pause] [long pause] one more time. [long pause] Your whole body at once. [long pause] Tense everything. [long pause] Fists, arms, shoulders, face, stomach, legs — all of it. [long pause] Every muscle engaged. [long pause] Every fiber holding on.

This is what carrying the burden feels like. [long pause] All of it, all at once. [long pause] The job, the family, the money, the health, the future, the past — all clenched in every muscle of your body.
Hold it... [long pause] [long pause] hold all of it... [long pause] [long pause]
And release everything. [long pause] All at once. [long pause] Like setting down an armful of stones. [long pause] Let it all go.

Stay here. [long pause] Don't rush past this. [long pause] Don't immediately start thinking about what's next. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] feel what your body is like when it's not holding anything. [long pause] The looseness. [long pause] The warmth. [long pause] The extraordinary quiet of a body that has stopped fighting.
This is what it feels like to set things down.
"Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest."

This is not a suggestion for the strong. [long pause] It's an invitation for the tired. [long pause] For the person who's been holding everything together by sheer force of will and is starting to crack. [long pause] For the one who says "I'm fine" forty times a day and isn't. [long pause] For you. [long pause] Right now. [long pause] Tired as you are.

Let's do a second, gentler pass through the body. [long pause] No more tensing this time — just attention. [long pause] Just breath. [long pause] We're going to move through each area again, and wherever you find residual tension, just breathe into it. [long pause] No force. [long pause] No effort. [long pause] Just warmth and attention and permission.
Start at the top of your head. [long pause] Notice your scalp, your temples. [long pause] If there's tightness left, breathe into it. [long pause] Let your exhale carry it away.
Your forehead. [long pause] The space between your eyebrows. [long pause] Your eyes, resting behind their lids. [long pause] Breathe.
Your jaw. [long pause] Your tongue. [long pause] The muscles along the sides of your neck, the ones that have been holding your head up all day. [long pause] Breathe. [long pause] Let them soften.

Your shoulders. [long pause] Even after the release, there might be something left. [long pause] Layers of tension, built up over weeks and months. [long pause] That's fine. [long pause] You don't have to undo all of it today. [long pause] Just breathe into what's here now.
Down your arms to your wrists, your hands, your fingers. [long pause] Each one. [long pause] Breathe warmth into them.

Your chest. [long pause] The space around your heart. [long pause] This is a place that holds more than stress — it holds grief, worry, love, all of it pressed together. [long pause] Is it open right now? [long pause] Guarded? [long pause] Heavy? [long pause] Whatever it is... [long pause] [long pause] breathe. [long pause] Don't judge. [long pause] Just accompany.

Your stomach. [long pause] Your lower back — another place that quietly carries everything. [long pause] Breathe into it. [long pause] Let the muscles soften. [long pause] Let the spine be held by the surface beneath you.

Your hips. [long pause] Your thighs. [long pause] Your knees and calves. [long pause] Your ankles and feet. [long pause] All the way to the soles, to the tips of your toes. [long pause] Breathe into each area as you pass through. [long pause] Leaving warmth in your wake.

Now let your attention expand to hold your whole body at once. [long pause] Feel yourself as one thing — one breathing, softened, present body. [long pause] Not a collection of problems. [long pause] Not a machine that needs to perform. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] a person. [long pause] Resting. [long pause] Held up by something solid. [long pause] Breathing air you didn't create.
The apostle Peter wrote: "Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you." First Peter, chapter five, verse seven.

Cast. [long pause] Not politely hand over. [long pause] Not carefully place at his feet with an apology. [long pause] Cast — like throwing a heavy net into the sea. [long pause] Like hurling a stone off a cliff. [long pause] Let it go with that kind of force. [long pause] That kind of abandon. [long pause] That kind of relief.

He can hold it. [long pause] It won't crush him. [long pause] The thing that is crushing you — he carries it like it's nothing. [long pause] Not because your burden is small. [long pause] Because he is that strong.

You can pick the burden up again later if you want. [long pause] You probably will — that's human. [long pause] But right now, in this moment, it has been cast. [long pause] And God isn't asking you to take it back. [long pause] He isn't checking his watch. [long pause] He isn't rationing his rest.
Let's stay here for a while. [long pause] No words. [long pause] No effort. [long pause] No working on anything. [long pause] Just the breath moving in and out of a body that has, for now, let go.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
If your mind has started planning again, started listing, started bracing — notice it. [long pause] And gently set it down again. [long pause] You're not done resting yet.
"For my yoke is easy and my burden is light."

There's a strange promise in those words. [long pause] Not that life will be effortless. [long pause] Not that nothing will hurt. [long pause] But that what God asks you to carry is not the thing that's crushing you. [long pause] The crushing weight — the performance, the control, the constant vigilance, the need to hold everything together — that's the stuff you were never meant to hold alone. [long pause] That's the yoke that doesn't fit. [long pause] God's yoke is different. [long pause] It's the yoke of trust, of surrender, of letting someone stronger carry the weight while you walk beside him.

Lord, this body is tired. [long pause] Not just physically — though that too. [long pause] Not just today's tired — a deeper tired. [long pause] The kind that comes from carrying things I was never built to carry alone. [long pause] From bracing against problems I can't solve by bracing. [long pause] From holding everything together while quietly falling apart.

You said to come. [long pause] You said you'd give rest. [long pause] So I'm coming. [long pause] Not with my life figured out. [long pause] Not with my stress resolved. [long pause] Not cleaned up or put together. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] weary. [long pause] And trusting that the invitation was real. [long pause] That it's still real. [long pause] That it was meant for someone exactly this tired.

Teach me to set things down more often. [long pause] Not because the work doesn't matter, but because I matter too. [long pause] And because you are strong enough — really, actually strong enough — to hold what I cannot.

Thank you for this body. [long pause] For its ability to tense and to release. [long pause] For the muscles that carried me today and the breath that keeps coming without my effort. [long pause] For the heartbeat I never asked for and the rest you offer without condition.
I receive it now. [long pause] Not because I've earned it. [long pause] Because you've given it.

When you're ready, begin to bring your attention back. [long pause] Wiggle your fingers, slowly. [long pause] Move your toes. [long pause] Take a deep breath — a full one, filling your lungs all the way up.

Feel the surface beneath you. [long pause] The temperature of the air. [long pause] The sounds of wherever you are. [long pause] The world is still here. [long pause] It didn't fall apart while you rested.

You can carry things differently now. [long pause] Not because the weight has changed, but because you've remembered that you're allowed to set it down. [long pause] That you were always allowed. [long pause] That the invitation stands — today, tomorrow, the next time the tension starts to build.

Move gently into whatever comes next. [long pause] And when you feel the bracing start again — the shoulders creeping up, the jaw tightening, the stomach clenching — remember: your body knows how to release. [long pause] You just practiced. [long pause] You can come back to this. [long pause] As many times as you need.
